steps.title: What Gather is
steps.narration: Gather is the front door for evidence. It pulls in web pages, feeds, papers, videos, and local docs, and it keeps a receipt for every source so the corpus can be checked again later.
steps.command: echo gather - accountable research intake with source receipts and a re-verifiable corpus
steps.stdout: gather - accountable research intake with source receipts and a re-verifiable corpus
steps.output: gather - accountable research intake with source receipts and a re-verifiable corpus

steps.title: The capability surface
steps.narration: One CLI reaches arXiv, RSS and Atom feeds, static web pages, PDFs, transcripts, and whole directories, all offline-friendly. Curiosity gets a well-organized intake pipe instead of a pile of open tabs.
steps.stdout:
usage: gather [-h] [--version]
              {status,doctor,demo,parse,video,web,feed,docs,arxiv,scholar,pdf,api,browser,ocr,transcribe,run,corpus,federation,caps,extract,markdown,crawl,mcp}
              ...

Gather: accountable research intake.

positional arguments:
  {status,doctor,demo,parse,video,web,feed,docs,arxiv,scholar,pdf,api,browser,ocr,transcribe,run,corpus,federation,caps,extract,markdown,crawl,mcp}
    status              emit Gather's Project Telos operator-spine status
    doctor              check Gather's operator-spine readiness
    demo                show Gather's operator-spine demo command
    parse               parse a saved yt-dlp info.json (+ optional .vtt),
                        offline, no network
    video               fetch a video via yt-dlp (needs yt-dlp on PATH and
                        network)
    web                 fetch a static web page via http(s) and extract
                        readable text
    feed                fetch an RSS or Atom feed via http(s)
    docs                read a local text file or a directory of them, offline
    arxiv               fetch papers from the arXiv API by id or free-text
                        query
    scholar             federate OpenAlex + Semantic Scholar + Crossref, dedup
                        by DOI, capture citation edges
    pdf                 extract text from a local PDF (needs pdftotext on
                        PATH)
    api                 fetch a JSON API with a bearer token from the
                        environment
    browser             fetch a JS-rendered page via a headless browser (needs
                        chromium on PATH)
    ocr                 recognize text in a local image via tesseract (needs
                        tesseract on PATH)
    transcribe          transcribe a local audio file via whisper (needs
                        whisper on PATH)
    run                 run a multi-source gather session from a JSON config
    corpus              inspect a stored corpus: list/verify/digest/runs/searc
                        h/stats/prune/availability
    federation          validate a registry, compile capture plans, or audit a
                        policy/entity receipt
    caps                report the web-data capabilities this install can
                        actually use
    extract             extract Markdown + a per-block provenance receipt from
                        a URL or local HTML file
    markdown            print structured Markdown for a URL or local HTML file
    crawl               crawl a site and emit a witnessed, hash-chained crawl
                        ledger as JSON
    mcp                 serve Gather tools over MCP stdio

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --version             show program's version number and exit

    caps                report the web-data capabilities this install can

options:

  --version             show program's version number and exit

steps.title: A status receipt
steps.narration: Gather reports its own state as a receipt. When your research rests on what a tool fetched, having the tool tell you plainly what it did is the difference between a claim and a footnote you can follow.
steps.stdout:
status=MATCH tool=gather command=status
next: index map - map workspace context for gathered sources
steps.output:
status=MATCH tool=gather command=status
next: index map - map workspace context for gathered sources
